Overview

Address
17803 IMPERIAL HIGHWAY, YORBA LINDA, CA 92886

Setting
Urban
4 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 4/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 3/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.93 5.19 3.67 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.64 0.91 0.47 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.65 1.15 1.23 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.64 3.13 1.97 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 2.29 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.12 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
5.10
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.57
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.79
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
35.2%
annual
RN turnover
Staffing penalized (missing PBJ submission).
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.84
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.33
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-07 16 14 2 60 1 60
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-01-26 30 30 0 120 1 120
Total weighted health score
75.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (57)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
53 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-25 2025-12-05
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. B 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. B 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. D 2025-04-07 2025-05-03
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-03-04 2025-03-17
0552 Ensure that residents are fully informed and understand their health status, care and treatments. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. B 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0693 Ensure that feeding tubes are not used unless there is a medical reason and the resident agrees; and provide appropriate care for a resident with a feeding tube. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0694 Provide for the safe, appropriate administration of IV fluids for a resident when needed. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-22
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0700 Try different approaches before using a bed rail. If a bed rail is needed, the facility must (1) assess a resident for safety risk; (2) review these risks and benefits with the resident/representative; (3) get informed consent; and (4) Correctly install and maintain the bed rail. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0802 Provide sufficient support personnel to safely and effectively carry out the functions of the food and nutrition service. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0803 Ensure menus must meet the nutritional needs of residents, be prepared in advance, be followed, be updated, be reviewed by dietician, and meet the needs of the resident. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0806 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food that accommodates resident allergies, intolerances, and preferences, as well as appealing options. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0813 Have a policy regarding use and storage of foods brought to residents by family and other visitors. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-22
0814 Dispose of garbage and refuse properly. B 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-22
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2024-01-26 2024-02-21
0909 Regularly inspect all bed frames, mattresses, and bed rails (if any) for safety; and all bed rails and mattresses must attach safely to the bed frame. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2024-01-26 2024-02-23
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2023-01-13 2023-01-24
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2023-01-13 2023-01-24
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2023-01-13 2023-01-24
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2023-01-13 2023-01-24
